docs.title: About me
docs.text:
I am a researcher in computer science at the Institute of Software Technology of the German Aerospace Center (DLR) and the Department of Computer Science, Humboldt-Universität zu Berlin, and a Fellow of the Software Sustainability Institute (SSI).

In the past I have worked as a Research Software Engineer in linguistics and the digital humanities projects, and as Managing Editor at Wiley-Blackwell.

docs.title: Research interests
docs.text:
• Research software sustainability
  • Research software citation & publication
  • Software metadata
  • Empirical software engineering
  • Evidence-based software engineering

For my PhD, I'm developing a way to measure the impact of dependencies on research software, to enable transitive credit and make hidden but important research dependencies more visible.

docs.title: Education
docs.text: I have received my Magister Artium (MA) in English (major), Modern German Literature, and General and German Linguistics (minor) from the Free University Berlin in 2010.

docs.title: Personal
docs.text: I live in Berlin, Germany, with my partner and our three kids.

docs.title: Activities
docs.text:
• Chair, ReSA Task Force "Software Authorship & Contribution" (since 2023)
  • Co-founder & member, Gesellschaft für Informatik (German Informatics Society) & de-RSE - Society for Research Software SIG "Research Software Engineering" (since 2023)
  • The Carpentries Certified Instructor (since 2019) - teaching researchers basics of research software engineering

docs.title: Past activities
docs.text:
• Co-founder & board member, de-RSE - Society for Research Software (2018-2021)
  • Member, FORCE11 Software Citation Implementation Working Group
  • Co-founder & delegate for Germany, International Council of RSE Associations (2020-2021)
  • Co-convener, Research Software Engineering Working Group, German Association for Digital Humanities

docs.title: Community service
docs.text:
• Organizer, Dagstuhl Seminar 24161 "Research Software Engineering: Bridging Knowledge Gaps", Schloss Dagstuhl, 14-19 April 2024. (Website, Report)
  • Steering Committee, "Software Sustainability Institute Collaborations Workshop", online, 04-07 April 2022. (Website)
  • Programme committee, "Nordic-RSE conference", 2021. (Website)
  • General Chair, "2nd International RSE Leaders Workshop 2020", online, 15-16+30 September 2020. (Website)
  • Steering Committee & Programme Committee, "SORSE - Series of Online Research Software Events", online, 2020. (Website)
  • Programme Chair, "de-RSE19 - Conference for Research Software Engineers in Germany", Potsdam, Germany, 04-06 June 2019. (Website)
  • Organizing Co-Chair, "Workshop on Sustainable Software for Science: Practice and Experiences (WSSSPE6.1)", IEEE eScience 2018, Amsterdam, Netherlands, 29 October 2018. (Website)
  • Co-Chair, Workshop "Research Software Engineering und Digital Humanities. Reflexion, Kartierung, Organisation." Digital Humanities im deutschsprachigen Raum Konferenz 2018, University of Cologne, Cologne, Germany, 27 February 2018. (Website, Abstract in Conference book of abstracts)
  • General Chair, "Workshop on Sustainable Software for Science: Practice and Experiences (WSSSPE5.1)", University of Manchester, Manchester, UK, 6 September 2017. (Website, Proceedings)

docs.title: Membership
docs.text:
• de-RSE - Society for Research Software, member since 2018
  • Society for Research Software Engineering, member since 2020
  • Gesellschaft für Informatik, member since 2019

docs.title: Project grants
docs.text:
Successful grant proposals for which I was eligible as an official applicant pre-PhD.

  • Principal Investigator, HERMES: Helmholtz Rich Metadata Software Publication, 2021-2023, Helmholtz Metadata Collaboration - Initiative and Networking Fund of the Helmholtz Association, project ZT-I-PF-3-006.

docs.title: Personal grants
docs.text:
• Incubator grant, Code for Science & Society Digital Infrastructure Incubator (funding phase: 2021-2022, USA; $5,000)
  • Fellowship, Software Sustainability Institute (funding phase: 2018-2019, UK; undisclosed); The Software Sustainability Institute is supported by the EPSRC, BBSRC and ESRC Grant EP/N006410/1.

docs.title: Awards
docs.text:
• RSE Catalyst (2022)
  • CampusSOURCE Award 2022 for Citation File Format (2022)

docs.title: Nominations
docs.text: • Society for Research Software Engineering (UK) - Outstanding Contribution Award Nominee for July 2020-June 2021 for promoting international RSE collaboration

docs.title: Projects
docs.text:
• since 2017: Citation File Format - A human- and machine-readable file format for software citation metadata (Inventor, co-lead, chair of the Steering Committee)
  • 2021-2023: HERMES - Automating software publication with rich metadata via continuous integration (PI)

docs.title: Past projects
docs.text:
As Research Software Engineer:

  • 2018-2022: Hexatomic, Friedrich Schiller University Jena: "A minimal infrastructure for the sustainable provision of extensible multi-layer annotation software for linguistic corpora", DFG, project number 391160252
  • 2016-2018: MelaTAMP, Humboldt-Universität zu Berlin: "A corpus-based contrastive study tense, aspect, modality and polarity (TAMP) in Austronesian languages of Melanesia", DFG, project number 273640553
  • 2011-2015: LinkType, Friedrich Schiller University Jena: "Towards a corpus-based typology of clause linkage", DFG, project number 199871864
